title: Family Dinner Night
headline: Feed the Whole Crew Without Emptying Your Wallet
body:
Tuesday night. Nobody wants to cook. The kids are hungry. You're tired.

Bring the family to Captains Fish and Chicken on 1st Ave NE. Our combo dinners are built for families — generous portions of fried catfish, shrimp, or chicken, plus fries and bread on every plate. Most dinners run $10–$15 per person, which means you can treat the whole table without the grocery bill, the dishes, or the cleanup.

We've got tables that seat six to eight comfortably. The dining room is clean, the staff is patient with kids, and the food comes out fast enough that nobody melts down. Our regulars bring their families every week. Some order the same thing. Others mix it up. Either way, they leave full and happy.

Dine in or order online through UberEats and DoorDash. Family dinner shouldn't be hard. At Captain's, it isn't.

faqs.question: Do you have seating for large families?
faqs.answer: Yes, we have tables that can accommodate groups of 6–8. For larger parties, arrive early or call ahead.

faqs.question: Is there a kids' menu?
faqs.answer: Our chicken tenders and catfish nuggets are perfect for kids. We also offer smaller portion sizes upon request.

faqs.question: Can we order for the whole family online?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Use UberEats or DoorDash to order family-size combos and have them delivered to your door.

faqs.question: Do you offer any family meal deals?
faqs.answer: Yes! Our Captain's Combo Dinner is the best value. Ask about seasonal family specials when you visit.

faqs.question: Is the restaurant kid-friendly?
faqs.answer: Very much so. We welcome families and keep the dining area clean and comfortable.

title: Game Day Grub
headline: The Food Your Watch Party Actually Needs
body:
Iowa vs. Iowa State. The Hawkeyes on a Saturday afternoon. The Cyclones in a bowl game. Whatever you're watching, you need food that matches the intensity.

Captains Fish and Chicken has the spread. Order a 20-piece wing platter, a seafood combo, and a side of hush puppies. The wings are fried naked and tossed in sauce — skin stays crisp even if the game goes to overtime. The shrimp is hot and juicy. The catfish doesn't get soggy on the drive home.

We package large orders in family trays so you can eat off the coffee table like civilized people. Pickup is fast. Delivery is available through UberEats and DoorDash. Call ahead for orders over $50 and we'll have everything ready when you pull up.

Game day food should be messy, crispy, and abundant. We deliver all three.

faqs.question: Can I place a large order for game day?
faqs.answer: Yes, we accept large orders. Call ahead for orders over $50 to ensure everything is ready on time.

faqs.question: Do you offer catering trays?
faqs.answer: We can package large orders in family trays. Call us to discuss your game day needs.

faqs.question: How fast is delivery on game day?
faqs.answer: Delivery times vary during peak hours. We recommend ordering early or choosing pickup.

faqs.question: What's the best item for a crowd?
faqs.answer: The 20pc wing platter and seafood combo are perfect for sharing. Add large fries for the table.

faqs.question: Do you stay open late on game days?
faqs.answer: We close at 10pm Mon–Sat and 9pm Sunday. Plan your order accordingly!

title: Lunch Break
headline: Fast, Filling, and Under $15 — Right on 1st Ave
body:
You have 45 minutes. Maybe 30. The drive-through line at the chain spot is wrapped around the building.

Skip it. Come to Captains Fish and Chicken at 1616 1st Ave NE. We're two minutes from downtown Cedar Rapids, easy to reach from I-380, and most orders are ready in 10–15 minutes. The combo dinners are under $15. They come with fries and bread. The portions are big enough that you might save half for dinner.

Our fastest items? Gizzards and fries — about 10 minutes. The catfish dinner is 12–15. If you're really in a rush, call ahead at (319) 221-1040 and we'll have it boxed and waiting.

Dine in if you want a quiet booth. Take it to go if you need to eat at your desk. Either way, you get a real lunch — not a sad sandwich from a vending machine.

faqs.question: How fast is lunch service?
faqs.answer: Most orders are ready in 10–15 minutes. Call ahead if you're in a rush.

faqs.question: Do you have lunch specials?
faqs.answer: Our Captain's Combo Dinner is always a great deal. Ask about any daily specials when you visit.

faqs.question: Is there parking nearby?
faqs.answer: Yes, we have convenient parking right outside our location on 1st Ave NE.

faqs.question: Can I order online for lunch pickup?
faqs.answer: Yes, order through UberEats or DoorDash for pickup or delivery.

faqs.question: What's the fastest item to order?
faqs.answer: Gizzards and fries are among our fastest items, ready in about 10 minutes.
